Wi-Fiの5GHzと2.4GHzを同じ名前にするメリット・デメリット: Pros and Cons of Using the Same SSID for 5GHz and 2.4GHz Wi-Fi
自宅のWi-Fiルーターで、5GHzと2.4GHzのネットワーク名を同じにする設: A clear explanation of how band steering (combining 5GHz and 2.4GHz Wi-Fi under one network name) works on home routers, plus common causes of instability and how to fix them.

障害物に強く、遠くまで電波が届きやすいのが特徴です。しかし、電子レンジやBlue: Good at penetrating walls and obstacles, so it covers a longer range. The downside is that it shares its frequency with microwaves, Bluetooth devices, and other household gadgets, making it susceptible to interference that can slow things down.
通信速度が非常に速く、他の家電の電波と干渉しにくいため安定しています。その反面、: Delivers much faster speeds and stays stable because it rarely clashes with other household devices. The trade-off is that it has trouble passing through walls and doors, so range is limited.
バンドステアリング機能を使ってこれらを同じ名前にすると、スマートフォンやパソコン: When you combine both bands under a single SSID using band steering, your phone or computer automatically connects to whichever frequency gives it the best signal at any given moment.
たとえば、ルーターと同じ部屋にいる時は高速な5GHzに接続し、そこから離れた寝室: For example, you might get the fast 5GHz band in the same room as the router, and then seamlessly switch to the more penetrating 2.4GHz band when you move to a farther room. No need to manually dig into Wi-Fi settings — it all happens automatically.
バンドステアリングのデメリットと落とし穴: Disadvantages and Pitfalls of Band Steering
一見すると便利な機能ですが、環境によっては逆効果になることもあります。: Convenient as it sounds, band steering can backfire depending on your home setup.
中途半端な切り替えによる「通信の途切れ」: Drops caused by indecisive band switching
スマホ側が「まだ5GHzで粘れる」と判断してしまうと、電波が微弱なのに2.4GH: When your phone decides it can still hold onto 5GHz — even with a weak signal — it won't switch to 2.4GHz. The result is dramatically slower speeds or "zombie Wi-Fi": full bars on the signal icon, but no actual internet.
スマート家電が繋がらない原因に: Smart home devices failing to set up
ロボット掃除機やスマートスピーカーなどのIoT家電の多くは「2.4GHzのみ」に: Most smart home devices — robot vacuums, smart speakers, and so on — only support 2.4GHz. If your phone is connected to 5GHz during the initial setup, the companion app often can't find the device, and setup fails.
Wi-Fiが不安定な場合の対処法: What to Do When Your Wi-Fi Connection is Unstable
ルーターの設定画面にアクセスし、「バンドステアリング」や「スマートコネクト」とい: Log into your router's admin page and disable "Band Steering" or "Smart Connect" to restore the two separate network names. In many cases, keeping your everyday devices on the fast 5GHz band and only switching to 2.4GHz for distant rooms or smart appliance setup results in a much more stable connection.
自宅のネット環境に合わせて、最適な設定を見つけてみてください。: Take the time to find the right configuration for your home network.
もし「同じ名前にしているのに通信がよく途切れる」「スマート家電が設定できない」と: If you're running into problems — frequent drops despite a merged network name, or smart devices that won't finish setup — the straightforward fix is to
バンドステアリング機能をオフ（無効）にし、5GHzと2.4GHzの名前を別々に分: turn band steering off and give the 5GHz and 2.4GHz bands their own separate names
自宅のWi-Fiにつなぐとき、「○○-G」と「○○-A」のように2つの名前（SS: If you've ever connected to home Wi-Fi and seen two network names (SSIDs) like "MyWifi-G" and "MyWifi-A", you've already encountered the two bands: 2.4GHz and 5GHz. More and more modern routers merge these into a single SSID using a feature called band steering. This article explains the pros and cons of that feature, and what to do when your connection becomes unstable.
スマート家電が繋がらない、または部屋を移動するとWi-Fiはあるのにネットが切れ: If smart home devices can't connect, or if moving between rooms causes the internet to drop despite having a Wi-Fi signal, we recommend turning band steering off and using separate SSIDs for 5GHz and 2.4GHz.
5GHzと2.4GHzの電波特性：知っておくべき決定的な違い: 5GHz and 2.4GHz Radio Characteristics: Key Differences You Should Know
5GHz帯（高速・干渉に強いが障害物に弱い）：: 5GHz band (fast and interference-resistant, but poor obstacle penetration):
障害物のない直線距離での通信速度が非常に高速で、他の家電製品と干渉しにくい性質を: Extremely fast in clear line-of-sight, and highly resistant to interference from other appliances. However, it is easily blocked by walls and floors, making it hard to reach rooms far from the router.
2.4GHz帯（低速・障害物に強いが干渉に弱い）：: 2.4GHz band (slower, good obstacle penetration, but prone to interference):
電波が壁などの障害物を回り込みやすく、遠くまで届くため、繋がりやすいのが長所です: Wraps around obstacles like walls and reaches far, making it easy to connect from anywhere in the home. The drawback is that it shares its frequency band with many everyday devices — microwaves, Bluetooth headsets, and so on — so using those appliances can cause connection instability.
バンドステアリングはこの2つを自動で切り替えますが、境界線上の部屋などで接続が不: Band steering switches between these two automatically, but in rooms near the edge of coverage this can trigger the "ping-pong effect" — the connection bouncing back and forth between bands and becoming unreliable.
